title: Exchange Client Access RPC Averaged Latency (Perfmon)
dataSources: Windows Performance Monitor `object=MSExchange RpcClientAccess` counter `RPC Averaged Latency` (typically `index=perfmon`)
app: Splunk Add-on for Windows (perfmon inputs) + on-prem Exchange Mailbox/CAS role
spl:
index=perfmon object="MSExchange RpcClientAccess" counter="RPC Averaged Latency" earliest=-4h
| eval ms=coalesce(Value, value)
| where ms>250
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(ms) as avg_rpc_ms max(ms) as peak_rpc_ms by _time, host
| sort -peak_rpc_ms
description: RPC averaged latency on Client Access servers reflects MAPI/HTTP and Outlook responsiveness; sustained elevation above Microsoft guidance usually indicates CPU, storage, or network contention before users flood the service desk.
value: Provides an early warning for mailbox connectivity pain separate from transport queue depth, helping teams split client-access incidents from mail-flow incidents quickly.
implementation: Deploy `perfmon` inputs on all Exchange servers running the MSExchange RpcClientAccess process. Confirm object/counter spelling matches the OS locale. Baseline per site; some organizations alert >200 ms. Correlate with database failover events and storage latency.
visualization: Timechart of avg vs peak latency, table (host × peak), single value (worst CAS).
